---
title: "Leafy Bladderwort (Utricularia foliosa)"
slug: "utricularia-foliosa"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623049887/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Leafy Bladderwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Fagales"
tax_family: "Fagaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Fagales", "Fagaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623049887/medium.jpg" alt="Leafy Bladderwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/coreytcallaghan">
coreytcallaghan</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342169448">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Fagales
-   **Family:** Fagaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=170084)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats.

## Natural history notes

An aquatic carnivorous plant characterized by its finely divided, leafy stems and tiny bladder-like traps used to capture microscopic prey. In Florida, it is found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats such as ponds, lakes, and marshes. It grows during the warmer months and produces yellow flowers, obtaining nutrients by trapping small aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/170084-Utricularia-foliosa)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2728)
